text:
GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY CLINIC — FOLLOW-UP NOTE

ATTENDING: [Gynecologic Oncologist]
VISIT TYPE: Established patient, surveillance/maintenance follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Routine follow-up. Ms. Okonkwo is a 60-year-old woman with germline BRCA1-mutated FIGO Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, currently on Zejula (nira) maintenance, approximately 27 months into therapy and roughly 2.5 years from initial diagnosis. She presents today for scheduled surveillance visit.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okonkwo is well known to our service. Briefly, she was diagnosed with FIGO IIIC HGSOC, germline BRCA1-positive, HRD-high by tumor assay. She underwent successful primary debulking with R0 resection, followed by 6 cycles of carbo/pacli (completed without major incident aside from grade 2 peripheral neuropathy and alopecia), and was then initiated on Zejula 200 mg daily as maintenance therapy. Her nira dose was reduced to 100 mg daily early in the course due to grade 3 then recurrent grade 2 thrombocytopenia — she has been on 100 mg since and has done reasonably well at the lower dose. CA-125 nadired to 18 U/mL at end of chemotherapy and has remained in the normal range throughout maintenance. Serial CT imaging has shown no evidence of recurrence; most recent scan at approximately 15 months on nira was unremarkable.

At today's visit, she reports doing well overall. No new abdominal pain, no bloating, no change in bowel habits, no new pelvic pressure or fullness. She denies nausea, vomiting, early satiety. She is tolerating Zejula 100 mg without difficulty. She notes mild fatigue, which she attributes in part to the medication but also to life in general — she says it is manageable and not limiting her activities. She denies headache, dizziness, or chest pain. Her blood pressure has been reasonably well controlled at home per self-report.

Peripheral neuropathy in bilateral feet has been stable — she describes it as mild numbness, grade 1 at this point, not interfering with balance or daily function. She continues duloxetine, which she feels has been helpful. No falls.

Of note, at a prior visit she disclosed that one of her daughters has tested positive for BRCA1. She has been in contact with a genetic counselor regarding family cascade testing. She reports this has been emotionally difficult but that her daughter is engaged with appropriate surveillance. We offered to facilitate any coordination needed.

No new medications, no new diagnoses. She saw her primary care physician recently; blood pressure was noted to be borderline on one reading, though it appears well managed today.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: mild fatigue (chronic, stable); mild bilateral foot numbness (chronic, stable, grade 1 neuropathy).
Negative: no fever, no unintentional weight loss, no new abdominal pain or bloating, no nausea/vomiting, no early satiety, no change in bowel or bladder habits, no dysuria, no vaginal bleeding or discharge, no new back pain, no shortness of breath, no cough, no palpitations, no headache, no vision changes, no easy bruising or bleeding.

---

PHYSICAL EXAM
Vitals: BP 128/80 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, Temp 37.0°C, SpO2 99% RA, Weight stable.
ECOG PS: 1.

General: Well-appearing, well-nourished woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Pleasant affect.
HEENT: Normocephalic. Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis.
Neck: No lymphadenopathy, no thyromegaly.
Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
Pulmonary: CTAB. No wheezes, rhonchi, or decreased breath sounds.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly appreciated. Midline and lower abdominal surgical scars well-healed, without tenderness or mass. No palpable omental thickening or nodularity.
Pelvic: Limited exam performed — no palpable adnexal masses or nodularity along rectovaginal septum. Vaginal cuff intact, no mass or lesion.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Sensation mildly diminished in bilateral feet to light touch in stocking distribution — unchanged from prior visits.
Skin: No rash, no purpura.
Neurologic: Grossly intact. Gait normal.

---

LAB RESULTS (TODAY)
CA-125: 24 U/mL (normal; baseline after chemo ~18; trending stable in low-normal range over the past year)
CBC: WBC 5.9 K/uL, Hgb 11.6 g/dL (mild, chronic, stable — grade 1 anemia, consistent with prior values), Plt 155 K/uL (grade 1 thrombocytopenia, stable chronic at reduced nira dose)
BMP: within normal limits; creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, potassium 4.1 mEq/L
LFTs: within normal limits

---

IMAGING
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 2 months ago (at ~25 months of nira) — no evidence of recurrence. Prior imaging at 6 and 15 months of nira likewise negative. No new imaging performed today; next annual surveillance CT planned per our standard protocol.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. FIGO Stage IIIC HGSOC, germline BRCA1-mutated, HRD-high — sustained complete remission, approximately 27 months on Zejula maintenance (overall ~2.5 years from diagnosis).
   - She continues to do extremely well. CA-125 remains stable in the low-normal range at 24. No clinical or radiographic evidence of recurrence.
   - Continued Zejula 100 mg daily. This dose has been effective and tolerated; no rationale to attempt re-escalation given her history of significant thrombocytopenia at 200 mg. We had a brief discussion today about the duration of maintenance — per current data and guidelines, continuation is appropriate. She remains on track.
   - Plan next annual surveillance CT in approximately 3 months (will coordinate through scheduling).
   - Patient counseled again today on symptoms that should prompt earlier evaluation: new or worsening abdominal pain, increased abdominal girth or bloating that persists, new pelvic pressure, unexplained weight loss, new back pain, or rising CA-125 on repeat check. She verbalized understanding and knows to call the clinic if concerned between visits.
   - Discussed that in the event of relapse — which we hope remains distant — she would have meaningful options, including platinum rechallenge if she remained platinum-sensitive and potentially clinical trial enrollment. We have flagged her as a candidate for appropriate studies. No active trial to discuss today.

2. Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy — stable.
   - Continues duloxetine 60 mg daily with some subjective benefit. No worsening. Will continue to monitor. No dose change indicated.

3. Mild chronic anemia and thrombocytopenia (grade 1, Zejula-related) — stable.
   - CBC values unchanged from prior. No intervention needed. Will recheck at next visit in 3 months.

4. Hypertension — controlled.
   - BP 128/80 today, satisfactory. Continues lisinopril per primary care. We noted a prior episode of grade 2 HTN attributed possibly to Zejula; BP appears well managed at this time. Will continue to monitor at each visit.

5. BRCA1 family implications.
   - Patient's daughter has tested BRCA1 positive. Discussed briefly; referred daughter to genetic counseling (ongoing). Patient appears to have processed this with reasonable equanimity. We remain available to discuss further if needed.

Return to clinic in 3 months. Labs (CBC, CMP, CA-125) prior to visit. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to be scheduled. Patient agreeable to plan.

Time spent: approximately 35 minutes, greater than 50% counseling and coordination of care.

[Electronically signed — Gynecologic Oncology Attending]
